name: COVID-19 Severity
summary: COVID-19 severity reflects host–pathogen interactions and population-level outcomes shaped by age, comorbidities, exposure intensity, and healthcare access. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Severe outcomes are less common in adolescents but occur; included for educational host genetics and exposure context only.

heritability_estimate.source: GWAS-based estimates for severe outcomes (varies by definition)

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Crowding increases exposure opportunity; outcomes depend on many factors.
Respiratory vulnerability context; evidence varies by cohort.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Host genetics studies include multi-ancestry cohorts, but ascertainment varies.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Effect estimates can change with variants, vaccination, and context.

references.title: The COVID-19 Host Genetics Initiative: mapping host genetic determinants of COVID-19 severity
Housing conditions and respiratory infection risk: overview (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

references.title: The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
